observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. recognize income and expenses in the Trust Fund books upon utilization of funds received from national agencies or any other sources, whether private or government, that are considered as non-exchange transactions and record entries relevant to the transactions; and b. prepare and submit for succeeding years the Statement of Financial Performance for the Trust Fund along with the other financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Management still failed to recognize expenditures and revenues in CY 2021 arising from fulfillment of conditions for which Trust Funds were allotted.

observations.recommendation:
Hence, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory
Committee:
a. in cooperation with all offices concerned, to adopt COA Circular 2020-006 for
the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPEs, including reconciliation
of accounting records versus physical count; and
b. to use the format of RPCPPE provided in Annex 49 of NGAs Manual for LGUs
and adopt a uniform property identification system for PPE.
We also recommended that the GSO prepare and maintain property cards for all
classes of PPE and ensure that these assets be validated during PPE physical
count.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to execute a Deed of Donation to the
recipient agency for the PPE items transferred to various public schools as listed
in Annex F.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to effect the journal entry to drop the items subject of the Deed of Donation upon
execution of the said deed and endorse the necessary documents to the donee
agency.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Municipal Treasurer (a) to prepare and submit to the Municipal
Accounting Office the Schedule of Unreleased Commercial Checks in time for
the closing of the books to enable the reversion back to cash in bank and
payable accounts of the amount of unreleased checks; and b) for the checks
which have become stale, send written notice to the payees for the existence
of checks and to coordinate with them to facilitate issuance of new checks;
and
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to furnish copies to the Audit Team of the
recorded Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) relevant to the adjustments of the
deficiencies noted herein to reflect the correct and reliable Cash in Bank
account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary entries of prior year adjustments for the understatement of
assets totaling P4,924,447.00 and for any related accumulated depreciation for the
unrecorded PPEs.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. recognize income and expenses in the Trust Fund books upon utilization of
funds received from national agencies or any other sources, whether private or
government, that are considered as non-exchange transactions and record
entries relevant to the transactions; and
b. prepare and submit for succeeding years the Statement of Financial
Performance for the Trust Fund along with the other financial statements of
the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary journal entry to present the correct year-end balances of the
affected accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality. Henceforth,
record under the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account the DRRM funds received from
National Government.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor requires the Municipal Accountant and
General Services Officer to immediately comply with the full implementation of
the perpetual inventory method in recording and reporting all procured goods,
supplies and materials for distribution and consumption pursuant to the
Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Office
to conduct a periodic physical count of inventory as required in Section 124 of
MNGAS for LGUs and submit the RPCI to the Accounting Office and copy
furnish the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the
supporting documents as required under Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No.
2012-001 before processing payments to suppliers and contractors and submit
immediately all lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification
and audit; Henceforth, comply strictly with Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No.
2012-001;
b. direct the BAC Chairman to comply with Section 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of RIRR of
RA 9184 on the required posting of NOA, NTP and Approved Contract in the
PhilGEPS website; and
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to require contractors to post the required
warranty security for completed infrastructure pursuant to the form, amount
and validity period required in Section 62.2.3.3 of the RIRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to:
a. judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF especially
those under the Mitigation Fund-Capital Outlay; and
b. reevaluate programs included under the MF-Capital Outlay in order to achieve
the goal/target of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk
reduction and management.

Hence, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor stringently require the conduct of annual physical count of the PPEs from the General Services Officer. After which, require the immediate reconciliation of the GSO report versus the Accounting records to facilitate the review of the noted variances and effect the necessary adjustments. Also, to incorporate the necessary disclosures in the notes to financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor was able to stringently require the conduct of annual physical count on Land, Infrastructure Assets, Buildings, and other PPEs. After which, require the immediate reconciliation of Accounting and Property records. In addition, the necessary disclosures were still not included in the Notes to FS.
observations.reason: The submitted RPCPPE was still incomplete as PPE were not included in the RPCPPE. Further, reconciliation of Accounting and Property records was still not conducted resulting to a net difference of P440,637,304.07. Necessary disclosures were still not included in the Notes to FS.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended the disposal of the unserviceable properties to make room for the existing serviceable properties in storage as well as to eliminate them from the records.
observations.managementAction: The GSO stated that the said unserviceable items are safekept in the storage room and not disposed because after their assessment, the value is too cheap thus making it impractical to proceed with the disposal process considering that publication expenses will be incurred. On the other hand, the unserviceable vehicles were donated to public vocational schools in Malvar for automotive experiments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. make the necessary entries of prior year adjustments for the understatement of income and equity totaling to reflect the correct amount; b. recognize income in the Trust Fund books upon utilization of funds received from national agencies or any other sources, whether private or government, that are considered as non-exchange transactions and record entries relevant to the transactions; and c. prepare and submit for succeeding years the Statement of Financial Performance for the Trust Fund along with the other financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Have drawn the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Municipality for the overall success in its implementation of the social amelioration program together with the 15 barangays and recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to provide justification on the result of the validation of the beneficiaries showing some ineligible beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: During the validation, MSWDO staffs closely coordinated with the 15 barangays of the Municipality. However, there were some beneficiaries who were found ineligible. The Municipality substantially complied with the rules and regulations on the receipt and distribution of funds as provided in the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 2020-04.
observations.reason: There were some beneficiaries who were found ineligible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, the General Services Officer and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure coordination towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording and reporting of all procured goods, supplies and materials for distribution and consumption pursuant to Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the implementation of perpetual inventory method and will be applied for the FY 2022 and the succeeding years. The GSO agreed to comply with the recommendation to conduct periodic physical count of inventory and submit reports to Accounting Office and Audit Team.
observations.reason: Management is still recording purchases of inventory as outright expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Municipal Treasurer (a) to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office the Schedule of Unreleased Commercial Checks in time for the closing of the books to enable the reversion back to cash in bank and payable accounts of the amount of unreleased checks; and b) for the checks which have become stale, send written notice to the payees for the existence of checks and to coordinate with them to facilitate issuance of new checks; and b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to furnish copies to the Audit Team of the recorded Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) relevant to the adjustments of the deficiencies noted herein to reflect the correct and reliable Cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: As of May 28, 2021, the Municipal Treasurer had furnished the schedule. As of December 31, 2021, unreleased checks amounting to P6,335,367.84 and P299,033.01, respectively, were not restored/adjusted to the Cash in Bank and appropriate liability accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer did not prepare the schedule timely; the Municipal Accountant recorded adjustments for some items but checks amounting to P6,335,367.84 and stale check of P299,033.01 were not fully restored/adjusted as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to review the approved plan and submit copy of the revised LDRRM Investment Plan, if any. Submit as well, the required reports for the receipt and distribution of donations in-kind to the Taal evacuees, otherwise submit justification on non-preparation and non-submission of the said reports.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, the MDRRMO and the Designated GSO saw to it that subsequent in-kind donations are accounted and reported consistently as prescribed in the aforementioned COA Circular. The Budget of DRRM Fund for FY 2021 was not fully utilized by this LGU due to Covid-19; funds used as standby.
observations.reason: Some supporting reports were submitted; other required reports were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to provide reconciliation on the noted variance. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ascertain the completeness of the real property units subject of tax and utilize the information system for its real property tax transactions.
observations.managementAction: The MTO is still in the process of updating their RPT system towards reconciliation of their RPT records. The Municipal Treasurer optimally utilize its new information system for real property tax transactions so that intended benefits will be realized.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer is still in the process of implementing the RPT system; reconciliation not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to declare the amounts paid under protest as free from protest and give due notice to the taxpayers regarding the finality of the resolution. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to draw necessary adjusting entries to effect the same thereafter.
observations.managementAction: Identify those accounts and corresponding companies that had not yet refunded the whole amount. The Municipal Accountant had sent notices to the concerned SB members and as of Dec. 31, 2021, P175,000.00 was refunded by four SB members.
observations.reason: Some amounts were refunded; balance remains to be collected/refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide justification on the above-discussed audit observation. The Municipal Accountant should strictly implement the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 97-002 for the grant and liquidation of cash advance, particularly with respect to the prescriptive period for the latter. She must not accept liquidation reports not duly accomplished for their processing. Finally, the Municipal Accountant must cause or order the withholding of the payment of any money due to the concerned employees.
observations.managementAction: Due from Officers and Employees under the SEF was still unliquidated. The payrolls for Job Orders are now disbursed by means of automatic debit arrangement. The Municipal Accountant caused collection of some unliquidated advances; payrolls for job orders now processed.
observations.reason: There are still unliquidated cash advances under SEF.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. GSO designate to prepare and maintain road networks property cards and Report on Local Road Network; b. Municipal Engineer to exhaust measures to provide the prescribed inventory, and c. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year; and d. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose all the required information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The GSO is in the process of coordinating with the Municipal Engineer and started with the conduct of road networks inventory, but has not yet completed the required reports. The Municipal Accountant prepared and maintained property ledger cards for Local Road Networks but has not yet furnished the required reports to concerned offices.
observations.reason: Municipal Engineer did not furnish the required reports; inventory started but not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to make final confirmation with the payees of their existing claims from the Municipality. Otherwise, revert the long outstanding obligations to the government equity with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan and supported with documented efforts and results of confirmation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer traced back the status of each of the long outstanding payables indicated in the schedule.
observations.reason: Some items traced; confirmation still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Market Supervisor to: a. collect outstanding receivable from stallholders; b. adhere to the provisions of the Market Code and recommend cancellation of Contract of Lease if stall owners are in default of paying rentals for three consecutive months; and c. submit monthly report on receivables from all stallholders to Accounting Office for appropriate recognition in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Market Supervisor is updated in the submission of delinquent payers of monthly rentals. The Market Code is applied and notices sent to delinquent stallholders.
observations.reason: Market Supervisor submitted updated lists and notices; some recoveries pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan refund the fuel subsidy of P438,000.00 and stop appropriating funds for fuel expenses in the absence of assigned service vehicles.
observations.managementAction: As of Dec. 31, 2021, P175,000.00 was refunded by four SB members.
observations.reason: Some SB members refunded portions; whole amount not yet refunded.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System to administer the HGDG test and PIMME in the PPAs, to strengthen the implementation and evaluation of gender-focused issues as required under JMC no. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, the Municipality reported that Client-Focused and Organization-Focused GAD programs were implemented and the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report was prepared showing expenditures of P11,991,830.10.

observations.recommendation:
Hence, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor stringently
require the complete and thorough conduct of annual physical count of the PPEs from the
General Services Officer. After which, require the immediate reconciliation of the GSO
report versus the Accounting records to facilitate the review of the noted variances and
effect the necessary adjustments. Also, to incorporate the necessary disclosures
in the notes to financial statements.

We also recommended the disposal of the unserviceable properties to make room for the
existing serviceable properties in storage as well as to eliminate them from records.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to draw adjusting journal entries to adjust the receivables account and
record the remaining balance of the liability accounts under the Deferred RPT and
SET Revenue as income.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. make the necessary entries for prior year adjustments for the understatement
of income and government equity totaling ₱8,883,764.97;
b. recognize income in the Trust Fund books upon utilization of funds received
from national agencies or any other sources, whether private or government,
that are considered as non-exchange transactions and record entries relevant
to the transactions; and
c. prepare and submit for succeeding years the Statement of Financial
Performance for the Trust Fund along with the other financial statements of the
Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to provide justification on
the result of the validation of the beneficiaries showing some ineligibles.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, the
General Services Officer and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure coordination
towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording and
reporting of all procured goods, supplies and materials for distribution and
consumption pursuant to Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• require the Municipal Treasurer (a) to prepare and submit to the Municipal
Accounting Office the Schedule of Unreleased Commercial Checks in time for
the closing of the books to enable the reversion back to cash in bank and
payable accounts of the amount of unreleased checks; and b) for the checks
which have become stale, send written notice to the payees for the existence of
checks and to coordinate with them to facilitate issuance of new checks;
• instruct the Municipal Accountant to furnish copies to the Audit Team of the
recorded Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) relevant to the adjustments of the
deficiencies noted herein to reflect the correct and reliable Cash in Bank
account balance in the financial statements.

Despite this, we commended the MDRRMO and the whole Municipality for its
cooperative effort to address the needs, not just of the whole Municipality but also of
those other LGUs, during times of calamity and disaster. Yet, for the noted
deficiencies, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO and
the Municipal Accountant to review the disbursements whether made in accordance
with the approved plan and submit the copy of the revised LDRRM Investment Plan,
if any.

Submit as well, the required reports for the receipt and distribution of donations in-
kind to the Taal evacuees, otherwise submit justification on non-preparation and
non-submission of the said reports. The MDRRMO and the Designated GSO to see
to it that subsequent in-kind donations are accounted and reported consistently as
prescribed in the aforementioned COA Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider General Services Office/Department created, with acquisition of a Department Head and two staffs who will focus on the task of conducting a complete physical count of PPE and prepare the prescribed report thereon showing complete description for easy identification and monitoring. The GSO maintains property ledger cards for its PPE to have reference for essential details of the properties acquired, especially the latter in its report of inventory count and have it reconciled with the accounting records after each annual count.
observations.managementAction: The GSO commented that they shall diligently comply with the recommendations and shall immediate solution to reconcile with the accounting records despite its limited manpower. The GSO shall conduct the disposal of unserviceable properties upon completion of its physical count.
observations.reason: Updated manpower. Skeletal department or workforce schedule. Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to ascertain the completeness of the real property units subject of tax, as well as the collections made, posting in its records. We also recommended that the Treasurer’s Office optimally utilize its new information system designed for its real property tax transactions so that the intended benefits expected therefrom will be realized.
observations.managementAction: Continuously in contact with the programmer taking notes of the expected output and what is necessary for the improvement of the reporting system formats are being polished and taking time to upload all the data from all barangays.
observations.reason: Since the RPT system is still new, the programmer taking notes of the output and what is necessary. Taking time to upload all data from all barangays.

observations.recommendation: In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Accountant to withhold those taxes arising from collections and apply this practice prospectively.
observations.managementAction: Taxes are withheld at date and were included in the remittances for the month of December, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BPLO require the submission of sworn statement of gross receipts/sales or certified copies of income tax return so as to come-up with an accurate basis for the computation of business taxes. The BPLO should also verify, assess and validate the undercollection and demand the collection/payment thereof. Finally, the BPLO and the Municipal Treasurer Office should levy and collect taxes and fees in accordance with the Municipality’s Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: Foregoing considered, we recommended that the BPLO and the Municipal Treasurer Office verify and validate the sudden increase in the business tax dues. The BPLO has verified and validated that the concerned businesses have paid the correct dues. The BPLO has sent notices and the concerned documents were attached.
observations.reason: Some clients were baffled with the sudden increase in the business tax dues.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to declare the collections free from protest and give due notice to the taxpayers regarding the finality of the collections. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw necessary adjusting entries to effect the same thereafter.
observations.managementAction: Foregoing considered, the Municipal Treasurer started with the encoding of data in their system. The Municipal Accountant has recorded adjustments.
observations.reason: Since it has reached the prescribed period under LGC 1991.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the mentioned accountable officers to liquidate their outstanding cash advances immediately; the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the DepED Office as to the assignment of the former school head for the issuance of demand letter and/or imposition of all available remedies; the Accountant to withhold payment of any money due to any concerned employees if necessary.
observations.managementAction: In partial compliance, the Municipal Accountant recommended the grant and liquidation of cash advances and recommend the withholding of payment where necessary. The Municipal Treasurer is scheduling to release the said payments. Only two (2) disbursing officers in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer, there are still cash advances being granted at the same time.
observations.reason: There are still cash advances granted at the same time. The concerned cash advance of P21,240.00 remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert the CY 2013 and 2014 unspent LDRRMF amounting to P71,813.18 to the general fund so that it could be used for non-calamity related purposes and adjust its Trust Liabilities account.
observations.managementAction: Foregoing considered, the Municipal Accountant had already reprogrammed said amount for disaster related purposes by reason of the Taal Volcano Eruption and Covid 19 Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: Given the limited manpower, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor task the acting GSO to conduct the inventory of PPE by account until all accounts are completed. Also, consider hiring additional manpower to augment the GSO staff.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer conducted a physical inventory of PPE as of December 31, 2019. A copy of the inventory report was forwarded to the Office of the Municipal Accountant and the COA. The General Services Department is created, with acquisition of a Department Head and two staffs who will handle Inventory and other functions in the Mayor’s Office.
observations.reason: The inventory report is incomplete since the acquisition cost is not indicated therein. The GSO admitted that the said cost is not indicated for reference.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to set-up and reconcile the RPT receivables and deferred income with the Municipal Treasurer's records and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started with the encoding of data in the system of RPT, however, as of yearend, encoding is not yet completed.
observations.reason: Encoding is not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. GSO designate to prepare and maintain road networks and Report on Local Road Network based on the report submitted by the Municipal Road Engineer; b. Municipal Engineer to conduct inventory and report; c. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the road networks and submit RPCPPE; and d. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose required information in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer started with the conduct of inventory, but has not yet completed the required description and cost segregation of road components. The Municipal Engineer had disclosed the required property information in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer did not furnish the required reports to the concerned offices; work is still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to trace back the status of each of the existing claims from the Municipality. Otherwise, revert the long outstanding obligations to the government equity with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan and supported with documented efforts and results of confirmation.
observations.managementAction: We recommended to make final confirmation with the payees of their existing claims from the Municipality. Otherwise, revert the long outstanding obligations to government equity with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: No response from the concerned individuals. The schedule indicated outstanding payables that required tracing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Market Supervisor to: a. collect outstanding receivable from stallholders; b. adhere to provisions of the Market Code and cancel lease if stallholders are in default for three consecutive months; and c. submit monthly report on receivables from all stallholders to Accounting Office for appropriate recognition.
observations.managementAction: The Market Supervisor sent demand letters to the concerned stallholders. Some stallholders did not pay and monthly reports were not submitted to the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Some stallholders did not pay the demand letters and reports on receivables were not submitted to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor require the concerned Sangguniang Bayan members to refund the fuel subsidy and stop appropriating funds for fuel expenses in the assigned service vehicles.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant required the concerned SB members to refund the fuel subsidy of P175,000.00 and four SB members refunded their amounts.
observations.reason: Some SB members have not yet refunded the remaining amounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LCPC to prepare the aforementioned reports to assess implementation of identified programs for the children of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO took charge in the preparation of the required reports, and submission of said reports to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System to administer the HGDG test and PIMME in the PPAs, and activate the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team so that they can formulate corrective measures in implementation of GAD.
observations.managementAction: The LGU activated the GAD monitoring and evaluation team. The GAD focal person administered HGDG test and PIMME.
observations.reason: Noted limited manpower that will focus on said matter previously.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Government to ascertain the legality and validity of both claims so both parties may make necessary adjustment on their proper books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Government and provided certification stating that the LGU has no outstanding payable to the Provincial Government as reflected in the books of the municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is waiting for the response of the Provincial Government and has furnished them a copy of the account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires that the GSO maintains
property ledger cards for its PPE so as to have reference for essential details of the
properties acquired, especially with respect to the amount. The GSO should also
indicate the latter in its report of inventory count and have it reconciled with the
accounting records after each annual count.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to provide reconciliation on the noted
variance. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ascertain the
completeness of the real property units subject of tax, as well as the collections
made, posted in its records.

Furthermore, we also recommended that the Treasurer’s Office optimally utilize its new
information system designed for its real property tax transactions so that the intended
benefits expected therefrom will be realized.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal
Accountant to withhold those taxes arising from Accounts Payable still outstanding
as of date and to apply this practice prospectively.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the
Business Permits and Licensing Office (BPLO) to provide justification or
explanation on the audit observation. We also recommended that the BPLO and
the Municipal Treasurer Office (MTO) levies and collects taxes and fees in
accordance with the Municipality’s Revenue Code.

The BPLO should also require the submission of sworn statement of gross
receipts/sales or certified copies of income tax return so as to come-up with an
accurate basis for the computation of business taxes.

Finally, the BPLO and the MTO should verify, assess and validate the noted
undercollection of P1,086,238.00 and consequently demand the collection/payment
thereof. The absence of documented action or information to counter the
undercollection shall mean the issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal
Treasurer to declare the P6,469,012.27 tax collections free from protest and give
due notice to the taxpayers regarding the finality of the resolution. We also
recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the Municipal Accountant to draw
necessary adjusting entries to effect the same thereafter.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to provide justification on the above-discussed audit observation. The
Municipal Accountant should strictly implement the guidelines set forth in COA
Circular No. 97-002 for the grant and liquidation of cash advance, particularly with
respect to the prescriptive period for the latter. She must not accept liquidation
reports not duly accomplished for their processing. Finally, the Municipal
Accountant must cause or order the withholding of the payment of any money due
to the concerned employees.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to revert the CY 2013 and 2014 unspent LDRRMF amounting
to P71,813.18 to the general fund so that it could be used for non-calamity related
services and to draw the necessary entries to adjust its Trust Liabilities account.
We also recommended that the unexpended QRF and MF once transferred to Trust
Fund be treated as one fund, disregarding their previous distinctiveness.

Given the limited manpower, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor to conduct the task the acting GSO to conduct the inventory of PPEs by account until all accounts are completed. Also, consider hiring additional manpower to augment the GSO staff. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider General Services Office/Department with sufficient staff that could focus on the task of conducting a complete physical count of PPE and prepare the prescribed report showing complete description for easy identification and monitoring.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer created, with a permanent employee and additional job order employee were assigned in the inventory of PPE, they were not able to complete the back tracking and checking of the inventory records within the target date due to the eruption of the Taal Volcano and the occurrence of COVID-19 pandemic. The MGSO targets to reconcile the two reports by the end of the 3rd quarter of 2020, in order to comply with the Manual of Property Custodianship.
observations.reason: The report is incomplete since the acquisition cost is not indicated therein. The Inventory Committee admitted that they do not maintain property ledger cards which carry essential details regarding the assets acquired by the entity. The said cards were not turned over to them by the previous committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to submit the correct Schedule of Amount Due/RPT Collectibles for the year to be set-up by the Accountant as RPT, Real Property Tax Receivables/Deferred Real Property Tax Receivables and Deferred Special Education Tax Receivables. Also, reconcile their reciprocal accounts with total tax collectibles and payables and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer started with the encoding of data in the computer system of RPT. However, as of yearend, encoding is not yet completed. The Municipal Accountant submitted only partial report. The MTO provided partial schedules and is in the process of cleansing the records on real property tax both in the land tax record card and the new computerized RPT system availed early 2020. The RPT system is being updated by encoding the payments as posted in the land tax record and vice-versa. They look forward to provide and maintain complete records on the RPT and its delinquencies by end of 2020, despite the limited number of personnel they have.
observations.reason: The MTO's computerized system crashed in 2019 and recovery for the former is still in process; encoding is not yet completed and only partial report was submitted by the MTO.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to prepare and maintain the road networks property cards with the required description and cost segregation of road components; municipal engineer to conduct inventory of road networks and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System; and Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose all the required information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer started with the conduct of inventory. The Municipal Engineer had started with the conduct of inventory, but has not yet completed the required reports. The Municipal Engineer had implemented measures to provide the required reports but has not yet furnished the required reports to the concerned offices.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer did not furnish the required reports to the concerned offices; inventory has been started but not completed and property ledger cards are still not fully prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to trace back the status of each of the long outstanding payables and make final confirmation with the payees of their existing claims from the Municipality. Otherwise, revert the long outstanding obligations to the government equity with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan and supported with documented efforts and results of confirmation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Sangguniang Bayan and started tracing back the status of the long outstanding obligations. Some confirmation work was undertaken but full reconciliation is still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Market Supervisor (a) collect outstanding receivable from stallholders; (b) adhere to the provisions of the Market Code and recommend cancellation of the Contract of Lease if stall owners are in default of paying rentals for three consecutive months; and (c) submit monthly report on receivables from all stallholders to the Accounting Office for appropriate recognition in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Market Supervisor sent demand letters to the concerned stallholders. The Market Supervisor started collection efforts and cancellation of contract of lease for delinquent stallholders was implemented in some cases. Monthly reports were not consistently submitted to the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Some delinquent stallholders did not pay despite demand; reports on receivables were not consistently submitted to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor stop appropriating funds for fuel expenses in the absence of assigned service vehicles and refund the fuel subsidy.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan members refunded P175,000.00. Notices for refund were sent to concerned SB members.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LCPC to prepare the aforementioned reports to assess implementation of identified programs for the benefits of the children of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Services took charge in the preparation of the aforementioned reports, and submission of said reports to COA. See Annex B for the copy of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point to administer the HGDG test and PIMME in the PPAs, to strengthen implementation and evaluation of gender-related issues as required under JMC no. 2013-01. We likewise recommended that the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team be functional.
observations.managementAction: The LGU activated the GAD monitoring and evaluation team. The GAD focal person implemented some HGDG tests and PIMME activities, but full implementation was constrained by limited manpower that will focus on said matter.
observations.reason: Limited manpower that will focus on GAD matters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Government to ascertain the legality and validity of both claims so both parties may make the necessary reciprocal adjustments on their proper books of accounts. The Municipal Accountant shall reconcile amounts and provide certification of reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Government to ascertain the reconciled amounts and requested certification. The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the provincial government and provided a certification stating that the LGU has no amounting to P152,316.99 as reflected in the books of the said accounts in the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor instruct the mentioned Accountable Officers (AO) required under his jurisdiction to liquidate their outstanding cash advances immediately otherwise the Audit Team will be constrained to issue a demand letter and/or cause or order the withholding of the payment of any money due the AO. The Accountant to coordinate with the DepED Office as to the assignment of the former school head, for the DepEd issuance of demand letter and/or imposition of all available remedies with regards to the latter’s outstanding cash advance. Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant sent demand letters to the concerned officials and employees. The Municipal Accountant coordinated with DepEd for issuance of demand letter to the concerned official. Some cash advances were subsequently liquidated while others remain unliquidated; the Municipal Accountant will continue efforts to collect or recover outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Some liquidation reports were not made within the prescribed period; some concerned officials/employees did not respond to demand letters and did not liquidate advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor direct the officials concerned to submit to the Audit Team, for review and evaluation, copies of contracts and purchase orders and all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution/issuance in order that Management could be informed promptly of any defects/deficiencies found therein, so that these could be immediately corrected and to facilitate the inspection of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials submitted some contracts and purchase orders for review. The Municipal offices implemented measures to ensure submission; however, not all documents were submitted within the five (5) working days as required.
observations.reason: Not all copies of contracts and purchase orders were submitted within five (5) working days from execution/issuance; some officials failed to furnish the required documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and compare the amount of collectibles and payables with the records of the Provincial Government to ascertain the legality and validity of both claims so that both parties may make the necessary adjustment on their proper books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant coordinated with the provincial government and furnished them copy of the ledgers for regarding the account. The Municipal Accountant provided a certification stating that the LGU has no outstanding payable to provincial government as reflected in the books of the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
Given the limited manpower, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor task the
acting GSO to conduct the physical count of PPE by account until all accounts are
completed. Also, consider hiring additional manpower to augment the GSO staff.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider establishment of a General
Services Office/Department with sufficient staff that could focus on the task of conducting
a complete physical count of PPE and prepare the prescribed report thereon showing
complete description for easy identification and monitoring.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
submit the correct Schedule of Amount Due/RPT Collectibles for the year to be set-up by the
Accountant as RPT and SET Receivables and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their
reciprocal accounts with total tax delinquency as prepared by the Municipal Treasurer and
effect the necessary adjustments.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. GSO designate to prepare and maintain road networks property cards and
Report on Local Road Network based on the report submitted by the Municipal
Engineer;
b. Municipal Engineer to exhaust measures to provide the Municipal Accountant
and GSO designate with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for other road projects which are still not accounted;
c. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road
network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the
Road Network System as of the end of the year; and
d. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger
cards for Local Road Networks and disclose all the required information for the
said property in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to make final confirmation with the payees of their existing claims
from the Municipality. Otherwise, revert the long outstanding obligations to the
government equity with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan and supported with
documented efforts and results of confirmation.

We recommended that the Mayor direct Market Supervisor to:
a. collect outstanding receivable from stallholders;
b. adhere to the provisions of the Market Code and recommend cancellation of
Contract of Lease if stall owners are in default of paying rentals for three
consecutive months; and
c. submit monthly report on receivables from all stallholders to Accounting
Office for appropriate recognition in the books.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members refund the fuel subsidy of
P438,000.00 and stop appropriating funds for fuel expenses in the absence of assigned
service vehicles.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the LCPC to prepare the
aforementioned reports to assess implementation of identified programs for the
benefits of the children of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System to
administer the HGDG test and PIMME in the PPAs, to strengthen the implementation and
evaluation of gender- related issues as required under JMC no. 2013-01.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Monitoring
and Evaluation Team functional so that they can formulate and suggests corrective
measures in the implementation of the GAD.

We recommended that the Municipality continuously adhere to R.A. No. 8291, R.A. No.
9679 and the National Health Insurance Act of 2013.

observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comments:
The Acting General Services Officer had started with the conduct of physical inventory of PPEs for the current year.

Management’s Comments:
The Acting GSO will coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to come up with road networks property cards. The Municipal Engineer had started with the conduct of road networks inventory and will exert effort in completing the required reports in the appropriate forms. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant will prepare and maintain the property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose all the required information for the said property upon submission of the required reports by the Municipal Engineer and Acting GSO.

The Municipal Treasurer commented that she will exert efforts in communicating with the payees/suppliers in order to facilitate replacement of said stale checks. In case no response was made by the payees/suppliers, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant shall request from the Sangguniang Bayan for their authority to revert said long outstanding obligations to the unappropriated surplus of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record all the cash advances granted as Advances for Officers and Employees and prepare adjustment for the erroneous entry made during the year and in the previous years for the outstanding balance as of December 31, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant had reclassified the account according to recommendation by COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record all cash warranty security posted by suppliers and contractors to "Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable" account and prepare/make the necessary reclassification and adjustments in order to correct the misclassification of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal treasurer had reclassified the account and recorded all the additions to the proper account. The accounting office had recorded all the additions to the proper account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the fund to any of the authorized government depository banks in compliance with DOF Circular No. 01-2017 and COA Circular 92-382 to ensure safety of the funds from unforeseen events, which may be brought by uncertainty in the banking sector.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal deposit account with the balance of ₱1,478,747.00 was already closed.

The lacking supporting documents for the transactions has been submitted to COA office.
The disbursement vouchers had complete documentation and the municipality see to it that complete documents are attached to the claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor direct the Human Resource Management Officer(HRMO) to document the hiring of services of the job order personnel through the hiring contract of employees indicating the duties, responsibilities and work to be done during the period, together with the required stipulations/conditions as stated under CSC Memorandum Circular No. 24 series of 2002 and CSC Resolution No. 021480.  The concerned municipal officials/personnel to submit job order accomplishment reports and other documents/justifications pertaining to the payment made to job order employees hired during the year. The Accounting Office to process and approve only claims with complete documentations.
observations.managementAction: The contract of employees service or its equivalent indicating the duties, responsibilities and work to be done were being prepared by HRMO prior to hiring of employees. The accomplishment report of employees were being prepared and already attached to the DVs. The Accounting office see to it that complete documents were attached to the disbursement vouchers before it is paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor direct the officials concerned to submit to the Audit Team, for review and evaluation, within five (5) working days from execution/issuance, copies of contracts and purchase orders and all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution/issuance in order that Management could be informed promptly of any defects/deficiencies found therein, so that these could be immediately corrected and to facilitate the inspection of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The inspection shows that there were signboards/public notices on the site of government projects by the team together with the Municipal engineer which shows that there were signboards/and or public notices on the site of government projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality follow strictly the law on the submission of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) within the period to give enough time for the Auditor to review, monitor and evaluate the action taken by the management on the recommendations.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted the APPSI for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to submit to COA Team, the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those for implementation during the year, as well as the information that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices have already been posted in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Inspection conducted on all on-going projects by the team together with the Municipal Engineer shows that there were signboards/and or public notices on the site of government projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor instruct the General Services Officer and other officer concerned to submit Request for Inspection/Notice of Acceptance together with copies of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) to the COA Office within 24 hours after acceptance and inspection of all the deliveries by the management.
observations.managementAction: Copies of acceptance and inspection report (AIR) for all deliveries were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor require the Municipality’s LDRRMO and/or the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare a monthly Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF and submit the same to the Audit Team within the period prescribed by the above regulation for immediate audit action so that corresponding result may be brought to the attention of concerned officials the soonest possible time for their immediate and appropriate action.

We recommended that the Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to stamp the disbursement vouchers and all of its supporting documents after payment is made to preclude the re-use of the same and prevent any possible irregularity that may result therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will send notices to the concerned SB members.

observations.recommendation:
We strongly reiterated that the Mayor direct the:
a) Treasurer’s Office/Inventory Committee to hasten the completion of the inventory
count of all the property, plant and equipment and exert more effort to reconcile
their records on PPE and conduct an in depth verification and analysis of PPE
accounts. Identify/investigate causes of the noted discrepancies in the balances of
accounts to bring the two records in agreement; and effect the necessary
adjustments in both records, if warranted;
b) Treasurer’s Office/Inventory Committee to apply more time to identify the
unaccounted/unclassified properties amounting to P54,373,083.56 and effect
needed reconciliation on either records;
c) Treasurer’s Office/Inventory Committee to expedite the process by which the
unserviceable properties could be disposed of, in accordance with the
aforecited rules and regulations, so that the balances of Property, Plant and
Equipment accounts presented in the financial statements would only
represent those assets that are in existence and servicing the needs of the
Municipality;
d) Treasurer’s Office/Inventory Committee to use and submit the prescribed,
properly accomplished and approved Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment (RPCPPE) and prepare separate RPCPPE for each PPE account;
e) the Accounting Office to submit to COA the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) and
other supporting documents used as basis of adjustments/ reclassifications made
on the PPE amounting to P36,301,790.68; and
f) the Accounting Office to review the account titles used in the preparation of the
financial statement and use only the recently prescribed chart of accounts under
the Philippines Public Sector Accounting Standards.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
 Treasurer’s Office to prepare and maintain road networks property
cards and to submit Report on Local Road Network;
 Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects;
 Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s
road network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical
Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year; and
 Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property
ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose the information for the
said property in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the
provision cited under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to evaluate and compare the amount of collectibles and
payables with the records of the Provincial Government to ascertain the legality
and validity of both claims so both parties may make the necessary adjustment
on their proper books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
 the mentioned Accountable Officers (AO) under his jurisdiction to
immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances otherwise the
Audit Team will be constrained to issue a demand letter and
eventually advise the former to cause or order the withholding of the
payment of any money due the AO.
 The Accountant to coordinate with the DepED Office as to the
assignment of the former school head, for the issuance of demand
letter and/or imposition of all available remedies with regards to the
latters outstanding cash advance.
Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all
cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the above-cited
rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to record all the cash advances granted as Advances for Officers and
Employees and prepare adjustment for the erroneous entry made during the year
and in the previous years for the outstanding balance as of December 31, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to record all cash warranty security posted by suppliers and
contractors to “Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable” account and prepare/make
the necessary reclassification and adjustments in order to correct the
misclassification of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to transfer the fund to any of the authorized government depository
banks and to current account the total time deposits of P1,478,747.00 in
compliance with DOF Circular No. 01-2017 and COA Circular 92-382 to ensure
safety of the funds from unforeseen events, which may be brought by uncertainty
in the banking sector.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and the officials/employees concerned to:
 submit the lacking supporting documents for the above noted
transactions to warrant the regularity of the payments made,
otherwise, the transactions will be suspended/disallowed in audit;
 see to it that brief and concise explanation on the nature of
transactions was correctly stated/properly disclosed in the
disbursement vouchers, and;
 review the completeness of the supporting documents of disbursement
vouchers before the same are paid and ensure compliance with COA
Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Mayor direct the
 Human Resource Management Officer(HRMO) to document the
hiring of services of the job order personnel through an individual
contract of service or its equivalent indicating the duties,
responsibilities work to be done during the period, together with the
81
required stipulations/conditions as stated under CSC Memorandum
Circular No. 24 series of 2002 and CSC Resolution No. 021480.
 the concern municipal officials/personnel to submit accomplishment
reports and other documents/justifications pertaining to the payment
made to job order employees hired during the year.
 The Accounting Office to process and approve only, claims with
complete documentations.

We recommended that the Mayor direct the officials concerned to submit
to the Audit Team, for review and evaluation, copies of contracts and purchase
orders and all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days
from execution/issuance in order that Management could be informed promptly
of any defects/deficiencies found therein, so that these could be immediately
corrected and to facilitate the inspection of the projects.

We recommended that the Municipality follow strictly the above
provisions of law on the submission of the Agency Action Plan and Status of
Implementation (AAPSI) within the specified period to give enough time for the
Auditor to review, monitor and evaluate the action taken by the management on
the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer and other officials concerned to submit to COA Audit Team the list of
all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are for
implementation during the year, as well as the information that the appropriate
signboards and/or public notices have already been posted in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2013-004 within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General
Services Officer and other officer concerned to submit Request for
Inspection/Notice of Deliveries together with copies of the Acceptance and
Inspection Report (AIR) to the COA Office within 24 hours after acceptance and
inspection of all the deliveries by the management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipality’s
LDRRMO and/or the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare a monthly
Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF and submit the same to the Audit
Team within the period prescribed by the above regulation for immediate audit
action so that corresponding result may be brought to the attention of concerned
officials the soonest possible time for their immediate and appropriate action.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to stamp “PAID” the disbursement vouchers and all of its supporting
documents after payment is made to preclude the re-use of the same and prevent
any possible irregularity that may result therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Inventory Committee to:
 Hasten the completion of the inventory count of all the property, plant and
equipment and exert more effort to reconcile their records on PPE and
conduct an in depth verification and analysis of PPE accounts.
Identify/investigate causes of the noted discrepancies in the balances of
accounts to bring the two records in agreement; and effect the necessary
adjustments in both records, if warranted;
 Apply more time to identify the unaccounted/unclassified properties
amounting to P54,373,083.56 and effect needed reconciliation on either
records;
 Expedite the process by which the unserviceable properties could be
disposed of, in accordance with the aforecited rules and regulations, so
that the balances of Property, Plant and Equipment accounts presented in
the financial statements would only represent those assets that are in
existence and servicing the needs of the Municipality;
 Use and submit the prescribed, properly accomplished and approved
Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE)
and prepare separate RPCPPE for each PPE account;
Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
 Submit to COA the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) and other supporting
documents used as basis of adjustments/ reclassifications made on the PPE
amounting to P36,301,790.68; and
 Review the account titles used in the preparation of the financial statement
and use only the recently prescribed chart of accounts under the
Philippines Public Sector Accounting Standards.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already started the conduct of physical count of the Municipalities offices so as to establish with finality the complete identification of each and every properties owned by the Municipality. submitted a partial report on physical count

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and compare the amount of collectibles and payables with the records of the Provincial Government to ascertain the legality and validity of both claims so both parties may make the necessary adjustment on their proper books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has been continuously coordinating with the Provincial Government relative to the said matter and that the remaining outstanding accounts pertains to balances which are long outstanding in the Provincial and Municipal books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that charges to the SEF are only those allowed under the SEF rules and to require the Local School Board to justify charges made against the SEF and to submit supporting documents to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already implemented 24 out of the 25 projects of CY 2016. For CY 2017, the Municipality implemented 24 out of the 30 proposed projects for CY 2017 because some of the Barangay Officials decided to realign the budget to other projects while one planned infrastructure program was discontinued due to property dispute.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Development Council, ensure that all projects funded out of the 20 Percent Development Fund be implemented during the year to ensure that the full intention of development would be attained by all its constituents at the soonest possible time.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, the Municipality implemented 24 out of the 30 proposed projects for CY 2017 because some of the Barangay Officials decided to realign the budget to other projects while one planned infrastructure program was discontinued due to property dispute.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all concerned municipal officials to adhere strictly with the guidelines set under COA Circular No. 2013-01 for the utilization of LDRRMF, and to stop the practice of charging to LDRRMF the expenses that are not related to and not supportive of disaster risk management activities. Administrative expenses should be charged to the respective appropriation of the Offices concerned.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already refrained from payment of non priority expenses to SEF, and ensured that expenses charged to LDRRMF are related to disaster risk management activities from July 2017- December 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the provisions of RA 7160 and National Budget Circular No. 563 in the appropriation and payment of membership fees and annual dues and to ensure that payments of such kind to private organizations will not be charged against the Municipality’s funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already refrained from payment of membership fees and annual dues to various leagues, and ensures that payments of such kind to private organization will not be charged against the Municipality’s funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to speed up the preparation of the RPCPPE for Special Education Fund and submit the same to the Accounting Office for reconciliation.  Also, enjoin the Municipal Accountant to finish the reconciliation in the General Fund and look into the discrepancies noted and verify if these were unrecorded/missing or unserviceable properties, for proper action /investigation/adjustment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has retrieved all documents available in her Office to establish the identities of Property, Plant and Equipment as recorded in the books of accounts maiuntained by her Office.  She claimed, however, that many properties were not fully identified considering that documents supporting these acquisitions are no longer available and there was no transfer of accountabilities from former Accountant.
observations.reason: See Finding No. 1, Part II

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to make immediate reconciliation of the properties recorded being maintained by said offices so as to establish with finality the complete identification of each and every properties owned by the Municipality.

Likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to document every process made in the identification of all properties recorded in their books of accounts, and if unaccounted or unidentified items still persists, then seek the assistance of proper authority to drop from the books of accounts the items that cannot be identified so as to rid the books of unreliable balances that is affecting the fairness of accounts presentation of Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements of the Municipality.

Also, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate conduct of physical count of all properties in the vicinity of the Municipal Hall and elsewhere to ascertain the actual existence of properties owned and as a guide in the reconciliation to be made with the Office of the Municipal Accountant, all for the accuracy and reliability of PPE accounts presentation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and compare the amount of collectibles and payables with the records of the Provincial Government to ascertain the legality and validity of both claims. If claims are fully documented and establishes regularity of transactions, then settlements/collections should be made by the Municipality.  For those undocumented collectibles, where after all diligent efforts that had been exhausted by the Municipal Accountant still disclosed zero basis, then proper procedures leading to write-off of these accounts may be considered.

As to the undocumented payables, where after conferring with the Provincial Accountant showed the absence of any documents to prove validity of claims, then the Municipal Accountant may seek approval from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of these undocumented obligations to surplus accounts, where it could be used as a source of funds for succeeding appropriation to some municipal programs or projects.

Said remedial measures would eliminate the books of accounts of balances that are not fully supported with valid documents to warrant continued presentation in the financial statements of the Municipality of Malvar.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop immediately the payment of meals, snacks, food expenses and other related expenses not among the priority list of expenses to be charged against the SEF, in pursuit and compliance with the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160, and its implementing rules and regulations as embodied under Joint Circular No. 01 s 1998 of the DBM, DECS, & DILG.

Further, require the Local School Board to submit to the Audit Team satisfactory explanation/justification on why charges of P200,968.48 were made against SEF though these were not among the priority expenses against SEF.  Otherwise, the amount of P200,968.48 may not be allowed in audit.

Henceforth, require the Local School Board to ensure that expenditures charged against the Special Education Fund for CY 2017 shall include only those among the priority list of expenses to be charged against SEF as provided under the Revised Guidelines on the Use of SEF under Joint Circular No. 01 s 2017, thus guaranteeing the welfare of school children and that SEF are being utilized only for the purpose for which it was created.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being also the Chairman of the Municipal Development Council to ensure that all projects funded out of the 20 Percent Development Fund be implemented during the year that these projects were proposed for implementation to ensure that the full intention of development would be attained by all its constituents at the soonest possible time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all concerned municipal officials to adhere strictly with the guidelines set under COA Circular No. 2013-01 for the utilization of LDRRMF, and to stop the practice of charging to LDRRMF the expenses that are not related to and not supportive of disaster risk management activities.  Administrative expenses should be charged to the respective appropriation of the Offices concerned considering that the LDRRMF was only for specific purpose not related to the usual operation of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the provisions of RA 7160 and National Budget Circular No. 563 in the appropriation and payment of membership fees and annual dues to various leagues, and ensures that payments of such kind to private organization will not be charged against the Municipality’s funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: 

a) the Municipal Accountant   to review the RPT and SET Receivables giving emphasis on the identification of properties which are tax exempt and /or owned by the Municipality of Malvar. The beginning balance of P2,905,565.56 and difference on recorded collections for the month of May, June, August and December 2015 and effect the necessary adjusting entries thru Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs); 


b) the Municipal Accountant to records the RPT and SET Receivables based on the certified list of taxpayers with tax due and demandable prepared by the Municipal Treasurer to avoid the recording of tax receivables of properties which are tax exempt and/or owned by the Municipality; and 



c) the Municipal Accountant and Municipal  Treasurer  to conduct periodic   reconciliation of the total RPT/SET Receivables and total delinquencies.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer are still in the process of verification and conferment with the Municipal Assessor on the full identification of real properties owned by the Municipality, those properties that are exempted from tax and those taxable ones due to voluminous documents involved, and of other duties and functions of their respective offices, that also need similar attention. 


At the beginning of year, the Municipal Accountant recorded in the books of accounts the amount of real property taxes due from taxpayers based on the submitted certified list of taxpayers submitted by the Municipal Treasurer, but since there has been no separation yet, records still contain those exempted from taxes.



Both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant are still in the process of reconciliation of tax receivables, hence no reconciled results yet.
observations.reason: The task involved longer period to accomplish.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct concerned officials to properly control/regulate the issuance of gasoline through a properly accomplished trip tickets and request for fuel.  

Ensure that all trips were properly authorized and for official purposes only. 


Require all concerned officials to submit immediately to the Auditor concerned the duly approved and fully accomplished trip tickets to avoid audit suspension.
observations.managementAction:
The issuance of gas slip is now in the control of the Municipal Mayor’s Office.  



Every utilization of service vehicles are properly supported by approved Driver’s Trip Tickets.

The required Trip Tickets have been submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Voluminous transactions

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal   Mayor request the Sanggunian Bayan to revisit the Municipal Ordinance Nos. 32-2013 and 55-2014 and provide adequate criteria/guidelines on the grant of burial assistance to nearest surviving relatives of deceased senior citizens particularly those grants exceeding the amount allowed under existing ordinance.  Consequently, strictly comply with the cited provisions to ensure uniformity on the grants.  Submit legal basis for allowing the grant of financial assistance in excess of the allowable amount.
observations.managementAction: The provisions contained under Municipal Ordinance No. 32-2013 and 55-2014 remains unchanged, but the Municipal Mayor has limited the burial assistance givcen to the relatives of deceased constituents to P1,000.00, while P2,000.00 were only given under extreme cases, which were few.
